event_info: lovely; short of a good length and at the pads; Sai Sudharsan leans across; lets the ball come and then clips it past mid-wicket for a boundary
short of a good length and well wide of off; slapped away down to third man for a single
beautifully played; short of a good length and just outside off - Gill stays on the back-foot and then powers the drive past backward point; races away to the square boundary
Mukesh shortens the length outside off - Gill stays on the back-foot and drops it wide of cover for a single
on a good length and just outside off - nudged off the back-foot past mid-wicket for a single
short of a good length and at the stumps; a soft leading edge back at the bowler - 11 off the over
creamed; overpitched and wide of off - Sudharsan leans across; lets the ball come and then caresses the drive past cover; runs away across the fast outfield
fuller length delivery on the pads; clipped into the gap past square leg for a single
short of a good length and well wide of off; punched off the back-foot to cover
tad fuller and just outside off; Gill presses across and checks the drive; back at the bowler
short of a length and angling into the right-hander; tucked off his body past square leg
the slower ball on off; Sudharsan drops it onto the on-side and is off for a quick single
shout for a catch at the wicket; but it's called as a wide - short and down leg; past Sai Sudharsan's attempted pull

ball_event: out Bowled!! Chopped on! Mukesh strikes early once again
event_info: shortened the length and pushed one across the left-hander - Sai Sudharsan's feet hardly moved as he rocked back on the cut; the bat comes down at an angle and he drags an inside edge; on the bounce and the zing bails light up. Sai Sudharsan b Mukesh Kumar 12(7) [4s-2]
on a good length and angling into the right-hander; clipped off the back-foot to mid-wicket
length ball at the pads; Buttler lets the ball come and defends it to the on-side
fuller length delivery outside off - Buttler leans into the drive and picks out cover
hammered; a floaty delivery around off; Buttler walks forward and then drives it through the line; a clean swing of the bat and the ball sails into the stands at long-on

KL has been smashed on his left middle-finger and he looks to be in pain - short of a good length and angling down leg; Buttler shuffled across on the flick and missed. The ball swung and KL didn't cover for it; gets smashed on the finger and the physio is out to have a look
on a good length and just outside off - Buttler stays back and pushes it in front of mid-off for a quick single
crunched; tossed up just outside off - Buttler prods forward and then goes inside out over cover; almost went all the way; bouncing in front of the ropes
massive; fraction too full from Axar - Buttler clears his front-leg and then lifts it through the line. A clean hit and the ball sails into the stands at long-on
pushed through on off; Buttler backs away and chops it in front of cover for a single
flighted delivery outside off - driven down to long-off for a single
tossed up just outside off; driven back at the bowler - Axar is late to get down and fumbles; allowing a single
tossed up on off; punched off the back-foot to long-off
cheeky and six; short of a length and at the stumps - Buttler shuffles across uses Mukesh's pace and then laps it over fine leg for a maximum
massive; that's into the final tier - overpitched by Mukesh; Buttler leans forward; not a big stride as he swipes it through the line; sends it high and into the stands for a 96m six
Buttler is looking in sublime touch; Mukesh follows Buttler as he backed away - but it's a nonchalant flick and he beats mid-wicket to his left
banged in short and at the stumps - Buttler backs away on the cut and missed
cracking shot; Buttler is dealing in boundaries at the moment - runs down the pitcdh; makes room to a full ball and then carves it high over cover for another maximum
banged in short and at the stumps - Buttler runs down the pitch and flat-bats the pull past square leg for a single; 23 off the over
slower ball outside off - drilled hard and straight to the fielder at mid-off
on a good length and just outside off - a punchy drive; but straight to the fielder at cover
the slower dipping full-toss; but it's still a tad too high and Buttler shovels it past the diving mid-wicket fielder for a boundary
this is much better from Ngidi - another dipping full-toss; Buttler is undone by the late dip as he leant forward on the drive; gets an inside edge onto the boot
on a good length and at the stumps; pushed in front of mid-on - Buttler wanted a quick single and is sent back
fuller length delivery outside off - Buttler reaches out on the stab to mid-off
flighted delivery on off; punched past the diving cover fielder for a single; brings up the 50-run stand
flatter and well wide of off; chopped square on the off-side for a single
tossed up on middle; Buttler goes right back and clips it into the gap wide of mid-wicket; comes back for the second and brings up Buttler's 50
flatter and at the pads; whipped off the back-foot to mid-wicket
beautifully played; Axar tosses one just outside off - Gill stays leg-sided and then goes inside out over extra-cover; couple of bounces and over the ropes
tossed up just outside off - a good stride forward in defence
tossed up and drifting across the right-hander; Buttler gets a soft leading edge on the tuck; wide of cover for a single
fraction short and just outside off - Gill rocks back and pulls it to long-on for a single

ball_event: out Bowled!! Cleaned up! Kuldeep slows down the pace and gets rid of the dangerous Jos Buttler - was dragged short and Buttler rocked back on the pull - the ball never came on and Buttler is through with the shot
event_info: swings across the line and misses it completely. The ball ball goes on to clatter into the middle-stump. Jos Buttler b Kuldeep Yadav 52(27) [4s-3 6s-5]
tossed up just outside off - Sundar goes back and punches it down to long-on
loopy delivery outside off; driven down to long-off for a single

drilled; Kuldeep gets too full and it's put away with ease by Sundar - gets a good stride forward; meets it on the half-volley and then hammers it back past the non-strikder. Beats the two men in the deep to a nicety
on a good length and just outside off - Gill plays from the crease and stabs it to the off-side
on a good length and just outside off; nudged square on the off-side for a single
short of a good length and well wide of off; punched in front of square on the off-side for a single
a slower floaty delivery outside off; driven hard and down to long-off for a single
on a good length and at the pads; nudged wide of mid-wicket for a single
banged in short and pushed across the right-hander Gill rides the bounce and stabs it wide of cover for a single
massive; too full and too straight from Kuldeep - Gill gets down low; picks the length early and then plays the slog-sweep; high and deep into the stands at backward square leg
flighted delivery on middle; worked down to long-on for a single
tossed up on off; Sundar goes back and tucks it past mid-wicket for a single
flighted delivery outside off - Gill backs away and plays a half-arm chop; placed wide of point for a single
flighted delivery on middle and leg; worked wide of mid-wicket for a single
flighted delivery outside off - steered wide of cover-point for a single. GT past 100 at the half-way stage of the innings
charges down; stays leg-side and gets a juicy full toss; swiped along the ground to long-on
118.9ks slower ball outside off; Washington cuts to backward point and there's a half stop; easy single
120.7ks off-pace delivery bowled into the surface; Gill drags the pull to deep mid-wicket
this is right where Washington likes it and he does not miss out. Natarajan strays down leg; Washington shuffles inside the line and helps it along over short fine with a pull
130.5ks full toss on the pads; Washington flicks to deep square leg
129.4ks low full toss on off; Gill toe-ends the clip to mid-wicket
fraction short and drifting in; Gill is cramped for room and short-arm jabs the pull; goes on the bounce to deep mid-wicket
this is a proper drag down and Washington puts it away. This was short and outside off; Washington rocks back and pulls it in front of square; picking the gap between deep mid-wicket and long-on

FOUR; almost a repeat of the previous shot. Vipraj is not getting his line right and is being punished. Washington uses the depth of the crease and nails the pull to the exact same region. To make matters worse; Vipraj has overstepped. Free-hit coming up..

Vipraj being taken to the cleaners. They can't change the field as it is the same batter; so Vipraj decides to go round the wicket; but the length is short again and it is easy pickings for Washington; he rocks back and hammers the pull into the deep mid-wicket stand
flighted delivery on off; Washington leans forward and works it to long-on

now Gill has a piece of Vipraj. He gets a full toss and Gill goes straight down the ground. Very minimal followthrough of the bat and the ball lands in front of the sightscreen. This is a momentum-shifting over..
massive over - 23 runs. This was the line and length Vipraj should have bowled all over. Tossed up around off; draws Gill forward and beats him with the away turn
88.5ks flighted delivery on off; Washington eases it to the left of Kuldeep and down to long-on
90.9ks short and drifting away; Gill chops the cut to backward point. This didn't bounce much
Gill launches Kuldeep into the stands. 90.2ks touch quicker and at the stumps; but the length allowed Gill to get under it - he kneels down and slog sweeps it towards deep mid-wicket
googly around off; Gill hangs back and knocks it to long-on
short and outside off; Washington cuts and picks out Mukesh at backward point
90ks drifting into middle and leg; Washington nudges it through mid-wicket
88.5ks sliding across from round the wicket; Washington eases it to long-off

did that carry? Excellent effort by Miller. Gill comes down the track; does not get to the pitch but goes through with the loft; hits it flat and towards long-on. Miller runs in and dives; gets fingertips to it before the ball rolls in front of him. He was charging in full speed and could not quite get his fingers under the ball
flatter and drifted across; Washington sits down and sweeps on the bounce to deep backward square
slows it up a bit around off; Gill waits and eases it to sweeper cover

Washington is nailing the pull shot. He is quick to pick the shortish length; clears his front leg and gets a clean swing of the bat; smashes it in front of square and beats the diving long-on fielder
tries to keep it away from Washington's range but can't keep it within the guideline; called a wide. Axar contemplates whether to review or not; in the end he doesn't
short and drifting away; Washington slaps it in front of square and calls for two; but they settle with a single
132.5ks back of a length angled across; Washington has a slash and is beaten on the outside-edge
112.3ks slower ball at the stumps; Washington checks his push and sends it to mid-off
shimmies down and is almost done in by the dipping slower ball; gets his bat down and toe-ends it to short fine
111.3ks dipping slower ball; Washington is deceived and chips it uppishly; the ball lands in the vacant mid-wicket region

off-pace delivery outside off; Gill shuffles across and lets it pass. They think about the review but Rahul is not convinced; so they don't send it upstairs. It would have been touch and go because Gill had moved across his stumps

fifty for Gill. Back fit and into the team; the skipper is leading from the front. He played second fiddle when Buttler is teeing off and now has stepped up himself. Raises his bat and soaks in the applause of his team mates. This is an excellent shot - another dipping slower ball; Gill bends down; takes it on the full and swipes it towards deep mid-wicket; beating the fielder's dive near the rope
another off-pace delivery from Ngidi; Gill crouches low and swipes it to deep mid-wicket
low full toss at the stumps; Gill flicks to deep mid-wicket
126ks full toss around off; Washington drives straight to long-off
117.5ks slower short of length delivery; sits up nicely for Gill and he does not miss out; gets on top of the bounce and nails the pull in front of square; one bounce and over the ropes

slower short ball outside off; Gill tries his best to reach it but doesn't connect. It was a close one but once again Rahul does not want it checked. In fact; Delhi Capitals have reviewed. They will have to check the line and height. The ball is on the line; some part is inside it. Is that enough? Yes. The decision is reversed. The height was fine as well..
low full toss; close to yorker length and Gill toe-ends the drive to long-on
132.9ks full pitcher outside off; Washington drives and picks out extra cover

that's a gift from Kuldeep. He went seam-up and down leg; 113ks short of length delivery; Gill stays inside the line and helps it along over backward square leg. There was pace on offer and Gill made used of that
googly outside off; Gill drags the pull to long-on. He was better off cutting that
85.1ks short and outside off; Washington chops it to point and quickly crosses over
second six in the over. This is floated up; 81.4ks one of the slowest Kuldeep has bowled; but it is overpitched and Gill bangs it straight down the ground. He sends it crashing into the sightscreen

Gujarat Titans lose a review. Googly that lands around off and spins away; Gill thinks it is a wide and decides to let it go. But the ball was within the wide marker. And even though Washington said no; Gill decided to challenge the onfield call and loses his side a review
excellent stop by Kuldeep. Wrong 'un that's short and outside off; Gill chops the cut to the left of the bowler. Kuldeep moves across and dives full length to save the single
112.3ks slower ball outside off; Washington drives wide of long-off and settles with a single
he's missed out there. Full toss outside off; Gill moves across and does not connect with the drive

ball_event: out Caught by Nitish Rana!! Ngidi breaks the partnership. A fine knock from Gill comes to an end. This is a classic Ngidi wicket these days. He has outfoxed the Titans captain with the slower delivery. The ball dips outside off and Gill is through the shot
event_info: he skies it straight up and Rana moves to his right near extra cover; settles under it and hangs on. This is a massive blow for the Titans as Gill had upped the ante and was looking dangerous. Shubman Gill c Nitish Rana b Lungi Ngidi 70(45) [4s-4 6s-5]
dipping full toss outside off; Phillips does not judge the speed on the ball and is through the drive early; misses and the ball bounces beyond the pitch
misses his radar and sprays it outside off; Phillips lets it pass and collects the wide
off-pace delivery; but the length is pulled back and Phillips knocks it down the pitch

what an over! Just 3 runs and the wicket of Gill. 112.4ks dipping full toss around off; Phillips drives it straight down the ground and has to be content with a single. An impressive spell from Ngidi - 4-0-24-1

uhhh! Phillips thought he had picked out the fielder. And was almost walking off; the bat slipped out of his hand. But the ball lands in front of Stubbs and Phillips has to sprint. He gets there and completes the single. Low full toss from Natarajan; Phillips miscues the loft and the ball goes on the bounce to long-on. Phillips lost his bat but is quick enough to cross over
full toss outside off; Washington moves across and laps it straight to short fine. He got himself into a tangle and could not quite middle the shot
speared into the blockhole; Washington jams it to short third and crosses over
129.7ks low full toss around off; Phillips drags the drive to long-on

finally a boundary. There was a lull in the last couple of overs and the Titans needed this badly. Fifty for Washington. His maiden IPL half-century and it has taken him a while. He raises his bat and soaks in the applause of his team mates in the dugout. Natarajan gifts his state mate a juicy full toss; Washington crouches low and heaves it in front of square; getting enough for the ball to land beyond the deep square leg fence

that's a good end to the over. Follows the six with a four. Natarajan misses his yorker by a couple of inches and that is enough; Washington drills it straight back past Natarajan and beats the two fielders converging in the deep
that's more like it from Phillips and the Titans. They are getting the move on. Length delivery from Mukesh and Phillips does not miss out; heaves it high and all the way over deep mid-wicket
135ks yorker with a bit of inward tail; Phillips squeezes it to extra cover and crosses over

ball_event: out Caught by Nitish Rana!! This is good bowling from Mukesh after being hit for a six first ball. This is a low full toss and Washington tries his best to get under
event_info: he even crouches low but all he could manage was a miscued loft towards mid-off. Rana moves around from extra cover and takes a simple catch. A fine knock from Washington ends in the final over. Washington Sundar c Nitish Rana b Mukesh Kumar 55(32) [4s-6 6s-2]

direct hit and Tewatia was gone. He had given up and not even in the frame. Very full delivery; close to yorker length and Tewatia digs it to mid-off. Phillips calls for the risky single and Tewatia responds. Axar swoops down and has a shy at the non-striker's end; but fails to hit the target

another bit of miscommunication. Fullish delivery outside off; right in the slot but Phillips attempts the reverse lap; misses and Tewatia is halfway down for a single; Phillips responds as well but they decide against it. Tewatia rushes back in

Phillips finishes off with a boundary. Mukesh misses his yorker and dishes out length outside off; Phillips throws everything behind the lofted drive and sends it bouncing over the deep extra cover fence
short of a good length and just outside off - Nissanka stays on the back-foot and punches it to cover
creamed; too full and too wide of off - Nissanka leans into the drive and plays it on the rise; finds the gap past cover and races away to the ropes
hammered; fuller length delivery outside off - Nissanka leans across; lets the ball come and then lifts it back over the bowler's head; couple of bounces and over the ropes
on a good length and just outside off - Nissanka uses Siraj's angle and runs it backward of square on the off-side for a single
on a good length and just outside off - steered square on the off-side for a single
smashed; fuller length delivery outside off - Nissanka leans across; holds his pose and then drills it back past the bowler; beats mid-off to his right and runs away to the ropes
fuller length delivery on the pads; KL whips and picks out the man at mid-wicket
fraction fuller and angling into the right-hander - KL leans across and defends it to the on-side
an effortless pick-up shot and KL has whipped it over wide mid-on; pitched up on middle; KL leans across and then dismisses it over the on-side; one bounce and over the ropes
short of a good length and well wide of off; slapped square on the off-side for a single
short of a good length and angling down leg - Nissanka shuffled across on the lap and missed. Jos Buttler isn't happy that it's called as a wide
short of a good length and just outside off - too close to cut and Nissanka skews a thick inside edge - Jos Buttler dives to his left to save some runs
beaten; short of a good length and angling across the right-hander - Nissanka throws his hands on the back-foot drive and misses
fuller length delivery on off; a high elbow as KL drove it back over the bowler's head; the timing isn't great and long-on gets across to keep them down to a couple
short of a good length and well wide of off; KL swipes across the line and misses
on a length and following KL as he came down the pitch; tucked towards mid-wicket
clubbed; fuller length delivery and well wide of off - KL lunges across into the drive and clears mid-off with ease; bounces away to the ropes
beaten; short of a good length and well wide of off; KL throws his hands on the drive and misses
short of a good length and angling down leg; past KL's flick and Jos Buttler does well to make a one-handed stop
on a good length and at the stumps; punched off the back-foot to mid-wicket
short of a good length and just outside off - Pathum Nissanka hacks across the line and the thick inside edge rolls behind square on the on-side
a high full-toss and it's a bad miscue as KL looked to shovel it over the on-side; the bottom-hand came off and the top-edge lobbed over square leg
another full-toss at 148.5 kmph; heaved behind square on the on-side for a single
that's too easy for someone of the class of KL; a leg-stump half-volley and he effortlessly flicks it over long leg for a maximum
back of a length and just outside off - KL stands tall and can't beat mid-off with the punch
another full-toss and KL tamely drives it to cover-point
he starts with the wrong 'un; but the length is short and Nissanka backs away on the cut to cover-point
tossed up well outside off - Nissanka bends down low on the cross-batted sweep past mid-wicket
fraction short and at the stumps; whipped down to long-on for a single
pushed through by Rashid - Nissanka rocks back and can't beat square leg with the half-arm jab
beauty; a ripping leg-break; drifts in and then spins away; brings KL forward and beats him on the outside edge
tad shorter in length; lets KL go back on the drive to long-on

that would have been a mindblowing catch had GP taken it; but he doesn't and can't prevent the boundary as well - short of a good length and just outside off - KL backed away and then steered it up and over backward point. GP ran a long way across; dived forward; but the ball bounced in front of him and then ricochetedd off his trousers and onto the ropes
fuller length delivery on the pads; whipped past square leg for a single
smashed; the slower ball and Nissanka picks it with ease - backs away and then goes inside out over cover with the drive; couple of bounces and over the ropes
smashed; fuller length delivery at 147.6 kmph; Nissanka sets a stable base and then hammers the drive over mid-off; went flat and hard; and all the way for a maximum
crunched; on a good length and just outside off - Nissanka stands tall and then hammers the shovel over wide mid-on; one bounce and over the ropes
cheeky and four; change of pace from Ashok; but there's width on offer and Nissanka runs it wide of backward point; 23 off the last batting PP over
short of a good length and just outside off; nudged past square leg for a single
fuller length delivery at the stumps; Nissanka looks to turn it onto the on-side; the extra pace beats him and the ball thuds high into the bat
fuller length delivery on the pads; clipped behind square on the on-side and Nissanka comes back for the second
fuller length delivery on the pads; past Nissanka's flick and Jos Buttler dives to his left to make the stop
short of a good length and just outside off - stabbed wide of cover-point for a single
hammered; on a good length and well wide of off - KL gets on top of the bounce and lays into the drive; one bounce and over the ropes at wide long-off
flighted delivery outside off - Nissanka makes room and cuts it square on the off-sdie for a single
Rashid tosses one just outside off - dropped into the gap wide of cover for a single
flighted delivery on middle; pushed down to long-on for a single
the wrong 'un from Rashid; spins back and cramps KL for room on the pull; an inside edge and the ball drops at his feet
tossed up on middle; a wristy on-drive by KL; on the bounce to long-on
the wrong 'un from Rashid; Nissanka plays with the spin and clips it behind square on the on-side

ball_event: out Caught by Rahul Tewatia!! There's the wicket and it's once again Prasidh Krishna who provides the breakthrough for the Titans. Was a back of a length delivery outside off and Nissanka was looking to play it on the rise
event_info: but it's Prasidh's extra bounce which led to his undoing - hit a fraction high on the bat and spooned a simple catch to Tewatia at mid-off. Pathum Nissanka c Rahul Tewatia b Prasidh Krishna 41(24) [4s-6 6s-1]
on a good length and just outside off - Nitish swings across the line and gets a thick inside edge past square leg
cracking shot; fraction short and just outside off - KL picks the length early; swivels across and then hammers the pull past square leg
beautifully played; change of pace and KL spotted it early; waits on the ball and then nonchalantly lofts it back over the bowler's head; sails over the ropes for a maximum
back-to-back sixes and KL is on a roll; pitched up by Prasidh and KL doesn'gt miss out; gets under it and smotes it over long-off for a maximum
short; wide and four; classy from KL; width on offer and KL goes hard on the cut; flays it behind square on the off-side for a 29-ball fifty. An expensive over from Prasidh; 21 came off it
tossed up on the leg-stump line; turned to short fine

Rashid strikes and it's a much-needed wicket for the Titans - unnecessary from Nitish; went down low and then shaped for the reverse sweep. Misses and is struck in front of middle. Did the ball catch the glove? He's reviewed it. Onto UltraEdge: A spike on UltraEdge when the ball is next to the bat. Definite edge and the on-field call has been reversed
pushed through quicker and at the pads; past Nitish's flick and strikes him on the pad
cracking shot; fraction short and bowled from behind the umpire - Nitish picks the length early; swivels across and pulls it over mid-wicket for a boundary

ball_event: out Caught by Sai Sudharsan!! A superb catch in the deep and Nitish's tentative innings has been cut short. Was the wrong 'un and the left-hander didn't spot it
event_info: went hard on the slog and mistimed it towards long-off. Sai Sudharsan ran in from deep; slid forward and completed a fine two-handed catch. Nitish Rana c Sai Sudharsan b Rashid Khan 5(6) [4s-1]

ball_event: out Bowled!! No heroics from the impact substitute - the wrong 'un and Rizvi is completely clueless. Was tossed up and spun back into the right-hander
event_info: drew Rizvi forward and he's pushing well away from his body; leaves a big bat-pad gap and the zing bails light up. Two fifties in the first two matches; out for a golden duck tonight. Rashid finds himself on a hat-trick. Sameer Rizvi b Rashid Khan 0(1)

back-to-back wickets does not matter for Rahul. First ball against Washington and he shimmies down the pitch; does not get to the pitch but goes through with the loft. He gets plenty of height but also the distance to clear long-on
this one stops a bit; turns in as well; Rahul checks his shot and sends it to the left of Washington
96.5ks sliding in with the arm; Miller stays rooted to the crease and squeezes it a thick inside-edge to backward square
102.1ks shortens the length; Rahul hangs back and punches to long-on
drifting into the pads; Miller nurdles it backward of square and scampers back for the second

appeal for LBW from Washington. Buttler gestures it is going down. Quicker and skidding in; Miller is cemented to the crease and beaten for pace; the ball goes past the inside-edge and crashes into the back pad. The angle was taking it past leg-stump
back of a length delivery; skids on and Rahul mistimes the pull to long-on
129.7ks slower short ball outside off; Miller is early into the pull and does not connect

tight single and Miller survives. 145.4ks short of length delivery; but the ball does not bounce; Miller toe-ends the pull into the off-side and Rahul calls him across. Phillips swoops down from cover and has a shy at the non-striker's end. Miller was scrambling and puts in the dive to make his ground. The throw from Phillips was on target
103.7ks off-pace delivery; Rahul waits and forces it down to long-on

100.6ks slower ball that goes horribly wrong; almost bangs it near his boot and the ball lobs away outside off. Miller does not bother. Umpire has a check if the ball landed within the playing area; it did and we carry on..
141ks back of a length delivery; Miller knocks it in front of mid-off and crosses over
110.1ks back of the hand slower ball; Rahul chops it towards extra cover and retains strike
136.8ks full toss angling in; Rahul whips it straight to deep square leg

140.9ks short of length angled in from round the wicket; Miller transfers his weight back and nails the pull to deep mid-wicket. No fielder there and the ball almost goes the distance. Miller is struggling with his finger - keeps shaking his hand after playing the shot
139.7ks short and angled down; Miller swivels and pulls straight to short fine. Prasidh got away there..
143.2ks full delivery and Miller uses the incoming angle; flicks it in front of square and picks up a couple
very full and close to yorker length; Miller clips it to deep mid-wicket

another costly over from Prasidh - 12 runs. Rahul continues to feast on his fellow state mate. Fullish delivery outside off; ample width and Rahul takes full advantage; he thumps it over extra cover and gives the sweeper no chance
93.9ks fullish delivery on off; Axar whips it along the ground to long-on
94.6ks googly around off; Rahul sits down and sweeps on the bounce to deep backward square
92.5ks short of length leg-break; Axar knocks it wide of cover and crosses over
95.2ks quicker and at the stumps; Rahul defends to the right of Rashid
93.4ks drifting into leg; Rahul clips it to deep mid-wicket

ball_event: out Caught by Glenn Phillips!! Rashid caps off his spell with a wicket. Excellent catch from Phillips. The change in angle works superbly. He throws up the googly and invites Axar into the big shot
event_info: the ball grips a little and spins away; Axar looks to go down the ground but the away turns means he slices the shot over extra cover. If the Titans had to pick one fielder to catch then it would have been Phillips. He turns around; sprints back and dives full length to take it with both hands. Axar Patel c Glenn Phillips b Rashid Khan 2(3)
starts with an in-angler down leg; Rahul misses the glance and it is signalled a wide
Rahul continues to motor along from one end. This is a slot delivery and he puts it away nonchalantly. Rabada misses the yorker and Rahul pumps his down the ground. No need to run for that..
127ks slower short ball; Rahul gets on top of the bounce and pulls towards deep mid-wicket
good length ball around off; Stubbs eases it to long-on
125.7ks another off-pace delivery banged in; gets big on Rahul and he miscues the pull; lands short and wide of Phillips at deep mid-wicket; just a single
142.2ks back of a length close to off; not quite there for the pull but Stubbs goes for it; the ball sneaks past his inside-edge
couple of dots to end the over. 140.8ks short of length delivery angled in; Stubbs misses the tuck and is struck on the thigh pad; the ball drops down on the pitch
boundary first ball. Ashok goes wide of the crease and slants the slower ball down leg; Rahul swivels around and helps the pull behind square. That was a poor delivery..
now sprays it outside off; another off-pace delivery that he does not get right; Rahul leaves and collects the wide
143.1ks back of a length around off; Rahul knocks it to extra cover and quickly crosses over

that's unfortunate. Ashok nails the yorker and Stubbs digs it to the left of the bowler. Ashok gets a hand but can't make a clean stop. The duo decide to risk a single. Gill swoops down; picks up the ball and scores a direct-hit at the striker's end. But Rahul was in and the ball deflects away for four additional runs

Sai goes for the catch and the ball bounces in front of him. Another slower delivery; outside off and Rahul has a swipe across the line. Not off the middle and Sai decided to go for the catch. He charges in and dives forward; but the ball lands short and deflects off him to the fence

Delhi Capitals lose a review. High full toss and Rahul was a bit surprised; he does not capitalise and just knocks it to long-on. But has a word with Stubbs and decides to review for a no-ball. It was clearly below the waist..
expensive over - 16 runs. 105.4ks back of the hand slower ball; Stubbs waits and still can't connect with the slash
low full toss outside off; swirls away a bit and hence Rahul toe-ends the slice to deep point. Washington almost overruns it but adjusts quickly and makes the stop

132.2ks full pitcher; right in the slot and Stubbs drills the drive to mid-off; goes on the bounce and Rashid does extremely well to make a half stop. He moves to his right and slides; gets his body behind and deflects it away; saved three
127.5ks slower short ball outside off; Rahul attempts the fancy reverse swat and does not connect

ball_event: out Tristan Stubbs Run Out!! This is a massive moment in the game. Rahul said "no
event_info: no; no; sorry mate but it was too late. Another off-pace delivery banged into the pitch; Rahul takes it on and toe-ends the pull to mid-on. He made a start for the single and then backed out. Stubbs was halfway down when he was sent back. He turns around and charges across; dives but is still well short of the crease. Sai does brilliantly to collect the ball cleanly and then fire a direct hit at the non-striker's end. It needed an accurate throw to catch Stubbs short and Sai delivered. Tristan Stubbs run out (Sai Sudharsan) 7(6)
full and outside off; tries to keep it away from Rahul's range but it is not within the marker; wide called
attempted yorker but Siraj does not keep it inside the guideline; Rahul leaves and pockets the wide

mid-on is inside the ring and Rahul takes advantage of that. Siraj does not get the yorker right; allows Rahul to get under and loft it over the infield. Phillips runs around from deep mid-wicket but can't make the stop

ball_event: out Caught by Jos Buttler!! Gone! Two wickets in the over but this is a big one. A well-set Rahul is walking back. No hundred for him. But what a knock! Started slowly
event_info: found his rhythm as the innings progressed and then struck it cleanly. Delhi Capitals needed him to stay till the end. Siraj gets the wide yorker right - this is full and outside off; just within the guideline and Rahul reaches for the drive; gets an outside-edge and Buttler does the rest behind the stumps. Siraj is pumped and is off on a celebratory run. Rahul c Jos Buttler b Mohammed Siraj 92(52) [4s-11 6s-4]
back of a length delivery; Miller mistimes the pull to deep mid-wicket
141.5ks back of a length around off; Nigam makes room and slices it over backward point. The ball lands safely and Phillips gets around from sweeper to keep it down to a brace
slower short ball; Nigam moves across his stumps and bends low to play the scoop; the ball lobs over him and barely carries to Buttler. The square leg umpire signals one for the over..
off-pace delivery; Nigam stays leg-side and flat-bats it to long-on
much needed boundary. Short of length angled across; Miller has enough space to free his hands; slaps it over extra cover and gets Delhi Capitals the four they were looking
127.3ks short of length at the body; Miller wanted to pull it backward of square but picks out the fielder in the deep. Just a single and he's disappointed with himself
yorker outside off; lands outside the guideline and Miller leaves; wide called

that's what Miller can do. He can clear the boundary with ease. Siraj goes slow and short into the wicket; Miller just delays his pull and manages to middle. The ball goes high and 82 meters long towards deep mid-wicket

uhhh! That's absolutely hammered. Siraj misses his yorker by a long margin; right in the arc and Miller does not miss out; he pumps it flat and over extra cover. The ball landed close to the ad cushions. The third umpire has a check and decides it is a four

oh my! Miller with the no-look shot. He's racing away with the game now. This is Miller at his best. Another length delivery; Miller keeps his head down and swings through the line. The ball flies off the middle and sails over long-on
off-pace delivery; banged into the surface; Miller makes room and pulls to mid-wicket. Phillips sprints in from the deep and keeps it to a single

Nigam is playing his role. This is the slower bouncer from Siraj; it seemed to be going over Nigam; but he backs away and telescopes it over Buttler. The fielder at third man is fine but not fine enough to stop the boundary. 14 from 7 now..
moves away and Siraj follows him with a full toss; Nigam tucks it backward of square and has to be content with a single. That's good for the Gujarat Titans. That was a poor over from Siraj - 23 runs

Nigam showing he is no mug with the bat. This is poor planning and execution. With mid-off and mid-on inside the ring; Prasidh goes full and outside off; Nigam stays leg-side and lofts it over wide mid-off; couple of bounces and over the rope. 9 off 5

ball_event: out Caught by Shubman Gill!! The chat does not work. Nigam goes. Gujarat Titans breathe again. It is not only a wicket but it also keeps Miller at the other end. This should have been the length on the first ball - short and into the pitch
event_info: Nigam makes room and reaches for the flat-batted hit; miscues it straight up and Gill backtracks near mid-off; steadies himself and hangs on safely. Vipraj Nigam c Shubman Gill b Prasidh Krishna 12(7) [4s-2]
that's good from Kuldeep. Prasidh goes round the wicket and angles it in; short of length and Kuldeep calmly opens the bat face; steers it to third man and brings Miller on strike. 8 off 3

in the arc and out of the park - that was the famous saying coined when Miller first came to the IPL. This has been deposited out of the ground. Another slot delivery; full and outside off; Miller has no half measures while playing these big shots; he lofts through the line and sends it all the way in the parking area at long-off. 2 off 2

he does not take the single. Short of length angled in; Miller swivels and pulls to deep square leg. Kuldeep was halfway down when he was sent back. Miller wants to do it himself. A single would have been the scores are level and even if Kuldeep had missed; it would have been a tie. 2 off 1. Chat between Gill and Prasidh. Tewatia joins in. Three men out in the deep on the leg-side.

ball_event: out Kuldeep Yadav Run Out!! Oh my! Gujarat Titans are celebrating. Kuldeep has been given out run-out. But Delhi Capitals have reviewed for a non-wide. What drama! It was a slower short ball
event_info: Miller missed the pull and the ball goes on the bounce to Buttler. Kuldeep has to go for the single. He does his best - runs and dives but Buttler catches him short with a direct hit. He gathered the ball cleanly and under-armed it onto the stumps. The dive was not enough for Kuldeep and the Capitals. The third umpire is checking for a wide. Miller's head height is 1.87 meters and the ball has passed at 1.75 meters; informs Saidharshan Kumar. It is a fair delivery and Gujarat Titans can continue their celebrations. Kuldeep Yadav run out (Jos Buttler) 1(1)
